name: universal_search
description: Performs ultra-fast recursive search across files and directories using ripgrep, returning strictly typed match structures ready for direct agent reasoning. No intermediate parsing required.

dependencies.name: node
dependencies.note: Required to execute the universal_search.cjs wrapper script.

dependencies.name: nunjucks
dependencies.note: Nunjucks template engine is required for argument rendering.

fallback.note: POSIX grep. Reduced output fidelity — no column offsets, no file statistics, no native JSON output.

pattern.description: The regex or literal string to search for.

path.description: The directory or file path to search within.

ignore_case.description: Perform case-insensitive matching. Defaults to false (case-sensitive) to match code search semantics and align with find_replace.

literal.description: Treat pattern as a fixed literal string rather than a regex. Overrides multiline.

word_regexp.description: Require matches to be on exact word boundaries (equivalent to \b in regex). Prevents partial matches inside larger words.

multiline.description: Enable multiline matching with dotall semantics. Allows . to match newlines and ^ / $ to match line boundaries.

hidden.description: Include hidden files and directories (those prefixed with a dot).

no_ignore.description: Search all files, including those usually ignored (like in .gitignore, build/, dist/, etc).

context_before.description: Number of surrounding lines to include before each match.

context_after.description: Number of surrounding lines to include after each match.

max_matches_per_file.description: Optional upper bound on matches returned per file. Prevents file saturation in minified files or large logs.

max_matches.description: Hard upper bound on total matches returned across all files. Enforced in the normalisation pipeline, not by rg (which only supports per-file limits). When reached, the result set is truncated and the truncated flag is set to true.

ignore_patterns.description: Glob patterns to exclude from traversal (e.g., '*.log', 'node_modules/', 'target/').

file_types.description: Restrict search to rg-registered file types (e.g., 'rust', 'py', 'json', 'ts'). Run `rg --type-list` for all supported types.

sort.description: Sort results by the given criterion. 'none' is fastest (rg's default parallel order). 'path' gives deterministic, reproducible output suitable for stable diffs and chaining.

truncated.description: True if max_matches was reached before traversal completed. Indicates the result set is incomplete.

used_fallback.description: True if the grep fallback was invoked instead of rg.

execution_ms.description: Wall-clock execution time in milliseconds, sourced from rg summary stats.

file.description: Absolute or relative path to the file containing the match.

column_start.description: Zero-indexed byte offset where the match begins on the line. Byte offset, not character offset — for multibyte UTF-8 content, convert using the source encoding before string slicing.

column_end.description: Zero-indexed byte offset where the match ends on the line. Byte offset, not character offset — for multibyte UTF-8 content, convert using the source encoding before string slicing.

match_text.description: The exact substring that matched the pattern.

line_text.description: Full content of the matched line, MUST be trimmed of trailing newlines (\n or \r\n) to preserve token density.

context_before.description: Lines immediately preceding the match, ordered oldest-first. MUST be completely omitted from the JSON payload if context_before is 0 to save tokens.

context_after.description: Lines immediately following the match, ordered top-to-bottom. MUST be completely omitted from the JSON payload if context_after is 0 to save tokens.

warnings.description: Non-fatal advisories emitted during execution (e.g., binary files skipped, ignored paths).

message.description: Human-readable description of the failure, safe for agent reasoning.

exit_code.description: Process exit code. rg exits 1 if no matches found (not an error), 2 on actual errors.

used_fallback.description: True if the grep fallback was attempted and also failed.

args: {% if multiline and not literal %}--multiline --multiline-dotall{% endif %}
{% for p in ignore_patterns %}--glob=!{{ p }}{% endfor %}
{% for ft in file_types %}--type={{ ft }}{% endfor %}
execution.note: No --max-count flag for global limits: rg only supports per-file match limits (which we expose as max_matches_per_file), not global. Global truncation to max_matches is enforced in the normalisation pipeline's truncate_matches step. --color=never and --no-heading are omitted as they have no effect in --json mode.

pipeline.description: Discard 'begin' and 'end' envelope messages. Retain only match data and the terminal summary.

transforms.trim_trailing_newlines: Strip trailing \n and \r\n from line_text. rg includes the line terminator in data.lines.text; the output contract requires it trimmed to preserve token density.

pipeline.description: Extract per-match fields from rg NDJSON match messages using rg's documented JSON schema. Applies trim transform to line_text.
Associate preceding and following context messages with their adjacent match by line proximity.
Extract aggregate statistics from the terminal rg summary block.

pipeline.condition: exit_code == 1 OR matches array is empty
pipeline.description: rg exit code 1 means no matches — this is not an error. Emit a valid success envelope with zero matches. Skip remaining pipeline steps.

pipeline.action: slice matches array to inputs.max_matches entries
pipeline.description: Enforce global match limit. rg has no global cap (--max-count is per-file), so truncation is applied here in the pipeline after all matches are collected.

pipeline.condition: truncated not already set AND total_matches > len(matches)
pipeline.description: Secondary truncation detection for edge cases where rg's own limits or timeouts caused incomplete results.
Merge all extracted fields into the success output envelope and strip rg-internal fields. Set truncated to false if not previously set.

output_gaps: column_start and column_end will be null
match_text isolation is unavailable (will return the entire line or null)

compatible_downstream.description: Pass match location directly to view the surrounding file content.
Feed matched file paths into ast-grep for AST-level structural analysis.
Pass match location and text to sd for targeted in-place replacement.
Read full file contents for matched paths using bat or cat.

compatible_upstream.description: Receive a directory path from fd or eza to scope the search.
Receive specific file paths from fd for targeted search.

vanquish.note: Match output is directly ingestible by Vanquish. Semantic fields carry sufficient signal for embedding without additional transformation.
